dosing_enrichment.titration_note: Begin Semax at 300 mcg/day (intranasal) for weeks 1–2 to assess tolerability. If well tolerated, may increase to 600 mcg/day in weeks 3–4 for enhanced BDNF stimulation.
dosing_enrichment.cycling_recommendation: 12-week active cycle followed by 4–8 week washout. Continuous use beyond 16 weeks not supported by current evidence.
dosing_enrichment.maintenance_dose: Semax: 300–600 mcg/day intranasal (morning). Selank: 300 mcg 3x/week intranasal.
dosing_enrichment.timing_optimization: Administer Semax in the morning (pre-work) for optimal cognitive activation. Avoid administration after 14:00 to prevent sleep onset interference.

protocol_author_notes: Official curated protocol for general clinical use.

qualitative: Improved sustained attention and task initiation
Reduction in cognitive fatigue and mental fog
Improved stress resilience and emotional regulation
Reduced cognitive fatigue and mental fog
Better cognitive consistency during high-demand work periods

quantitative_ranges.week_8: Working memory composite scores improve ~15–20% vs baseline; HAM-A anxiety score reduction of 30–40% with Selank (Zozulya 2014)
quantitative_ranges.week_4: 60–70% of subjects report subjective focus improvement; VAS cognitive fatigue score reduction of 25–35% (Akhapkina 2002)
quantitative_ranges.week_12: Sustained cognitive enhancement in ~65% of responders; BDNF plasma levels increase 20–30% from Semax (Dolotov 2006)

expected_outcomes.responder_rate_pct: 65–70% for cognitive endpoints; ~60% for anxiety/stress endpoints

clinical_events.title: Cognitive response and sleep tolerance review

monitoringSchedule.label: End-of-Protocol Evaluation
monitoringSchedule.notes: Full outcome assessment and decision on continuation or cycling.

side_effects.management: Rotate injection sites; use 29–31G insulin needles; apply cold compress.
Administer with food; reduce dose temporarily if persistent.
Adjust injection timing; evaluate sleep hygiene.
Hydration; paracetamol as needed; review dosing.

escalation_criteria: Allergic reaction (urticaria, angioedema, anaphylaxis) — STOP immediately, administer epinephrine if severe
Persistent injection site infection (redness, warmth, pus) — STOP, antibiotics, dermatology review
Significant mood deterioration or psychotic symptoms — STOP, psychiatric referral
Unexplained abnormal labs > 2× upper limit of normal — STOP, specialist review
riskManagement.storage_handling: Store peptides at 2–8°C (refrigerated). Reconstituted vials: stable for 28 days refrigerated. Do not freeze reconstituted product. Protect from light.
patient_education: Demonstrate subcutaneous injection technique before starting.
Provide written dosing schedule and injection site rotation map.
Instruct patient to report any unusual symptoms within 24h.
Provide emergency contact number for out-of-hours concerns.

clinical_disclaimer: For use by qualified healthcare professionals only. Not a diagnosis, prescription, or substitute for individualized medical judgment, informed consent, and local regulatory compliance.

evidence_limitations: Several peptide uses in this library remain off-label, investigational, exploratory, or based on translational evidence.
Protocol data should not be interpreted as individualized medical advice or a substitute for prescribing clinician judgment.
Regulatory status varies by country and must be checked locally before clinical use or commercial distribution.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.update_scope: missing clinical/regulatory/safety information added while preserving canonical antigravity_v2 data model
latest_clinical_notes: Semax and Selank remain region-specific/exploratory outside Russia and selected markets.
Cognitive protocols should screen for anxiety severity, bipolar/psychosis history, seizure risk, stimulant use, sleep impairment, and blood pressure.
Evidence remains less robust than approved neuropsychiatric therapies; use conservative claims.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.clinical_use_guardrail: Use under qualified medical supervision where legally permitted; obtain informed consent for off-label or investigational use.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.regulatory_status: Semax, Selank, and Pinealon are not FDA- or EMA-approved. Evidence base remains mainly Russian/regional clinical literature plus preclinical/translational studies.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.evidence_update: Maintain evidence grade C/exploratory-translational and avoid language implying established Western regulatory approval.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.clinical_use_note: This protocol is informational and designed for clinician-supervised review. It is not a standalone prescription, diagnosis, or patient-specific treatment plan.

contraindications: No broad Western post-marketing safety database; discontinue and review if neurological, psychiatric, or cardiovascular symptoms worsen.

legacy_compatibility.notes: Legacy fields retained for backward compatibility; phase_blueprints are preferred when present.

generator_rules.notes: Default generator rules – pending clinical review.

metadata.longDescription: Semax (Met-Glu-His-Phe-Pro-Gly-Pro) is a heptapeptide analogue of the ACTH(4-10) fragment developed by the Institute of Molecular Genetics (Russia). It upregulates BDNF and NGF in the hippocampus, enhances dopaminergic and serotonergic neurotransmission, and increases regional cerebral blood flow without stimulant side-effects (Dolotov OV et al., J Neurochem 2006; Eremin KO et al., Bull Exp Biol Med 2005). Selank (Thr-Lys-Pro-Arg-Pro-Gly-Pro) is a synthetic analogue of the immunomodulatory tetrapeptide tuftsin that potentiates GABAergic inhibition, reduces CRF-driven HPA axis hyperactivation, and upregulates BDNF, producing anxiolytic and cognitive-enhancing effects without dependence (Semenova TP et al., Bull Exp Biol Med 2010; Medvedev VE et al., Ther Arch 2015). The combination addresses the anxiety–cognition dyad: Selank reduces stress-induced cognitive interference while Semax directly augments executive function and memory consolidation.
metadata.target_patient_profile: Adults 25–60 with cognitive fatigue, high stress load, or mild executive function concerns. Suited for high-demand professionals, post-burnout recovery, or early cognitive decline prevention. Particularly indicated in individuals reporting poor task initiation, mental fog, and stress-driven attention impairment.
metadata.short_description: A 12-week peptide-based protocol targeting neurocognitive activation, working memory support, and stress resilience using Semax and Selank.
metadata.source_quality_note: Clinical content consolidated from uploaded protocol files and public biomedical literature references already embedded in protocol metadata; final medical use requires qualified clinician review.

references.citation: Dolotov OV et al. Semax stimulates BDNF expression in the mouse brain. J Neurochem 2006;97(Suppl 1):82-86.
Eremin KO et al. Semax activates brain dopaminergic systems. Bull Exp Biol Med 2005;140:274-276.
Semenova TP et al. Selank anxiolytic effects. Bull Exp Biol Med 2010;150:65-67.

evidence_notes: Use as a research-oriented cognitive support blueprint.
Human clinical literature is narrower and region-specific compared with endocrine/metabolic peptides.

metadata.scientificName: Semax & Selank ACTH/MSH-Derived Nootropic & Anxiolytic Protocol
metadata.description: 6-week intranasal neurocognitive protocol combining Semax BDNF/ACTH analog nootropism with Selank anxiolytic tuftsin-derived GABAergic modulation for focus, memory, and stress resilience.
metadata.evidence_grade_note: Strong preclinical evidence and multiple Russian Phase II/III clinical trials. Limited large-scale Western RCTs. Grade C per GRADE criteria due to regional publication bias.
metadata.clinical_summary: Semax (ACTH(4-7)-Pro-Gly-Pro analog) enhances BDNF expression and potentiates dopaminergic and serotonergic neurotransmission, producing measurable improvements in attention, working memory, and processing speed. Selank (tuftsin analog) modulates GABAergic and enkephalinergic signaling to attenuate stress-induced cognitive interference. Used together, Semax drives neurocognitive activation while Selank stabilizes emotional reactivity, creating a complementary protocol for cognitive optimization under high-demand conditions.

pubmed_references.title: The spectrum of pharmacological activity of neuropeptide Semax
Semax, an analogue of ACTH(4-7) with cognitive effects: preclinical and clinical data
The Selank peptide: research results and prospects
Effects of Selank on anxiety in rodent models

metadata.regulatory_note: Not FDA-approved. Research peptide in the US; available via 503A compounding pharmacies under physician supervision. Approved prescription in Russia for cognitive disorders and stroke rehabilitation. Not EMA-approved.

drug_interactions: Theoretical potentiation with CNS stimulants (caffeine, modafinil) — monitor for overstimulation
Avoid concurrent use with MAO inhibitors (theoretical serotonergic interaction)
Selank may potentiate sedative effects of anxiolytic medications
monitoring_required: Subjective cognitive VAS score (0–10) at weeks 0, 4, 8, 12
Blood pressure monitoring — Semax mild vasoconstrictive potential
Sleep quality assessment at each checkpoint
HAM-A or GAD-7 anxiety score at baseline and week 8 for Selank response tracking
adverse_events_serious: No serious adverse events reported in published clinical literature at standard doses (300–600 mcg/day)
adverse_events_common: Mild nasal irritation or epistaxis (15–20%) from intranasal delivery
Transient headache, especially in first 1–2 weeks (8–12%)
Mild stimulation or restlessness at higher Semax doses (5–10%)
Transient sleep onset delay if Semax administered after 14:00

scheduled_checkpoints.purpose: Assess early CNS tolerability and dosing suitability.
Evaluate initial cognitive and stress-response signal.
Determine whether washout, continuation, or protocol change is appropriate.
